description: Strong STEM entrepreneur — biotech founder with PhD, VC funding, patents, SBIR grant
input: Dr. Maria Chen, PhD in Biomedical Engineering from MIT (2018). Founded BioSense Diagnostics in 2020, a point-of-care diagnostic platform using novel microfluidic chip design for rapid pathogen detection. $2.5M Series A from top-tier VC. 3 granted patents. SBIR Phase II grant ($1.2M) from NIH. FDA breakthrough device designation. Platform deployed in 12 hospitals. 15 employees. Revenue: $800K in 2024, projecting $3M in 2025. Published 6 papers in Lab on a Chip and Biosensors & Bioelectronics with 180 citations. CDC expressed interest in pandemic preparedness application.
expected_behavior: Should produce QUALIFIED or LIKELY_QUALIFIED verdict. Tier 1 field (Biotechnology/Public Health). Strong across all evidence categories: ownership, IP, government support (SBIR), investment, publications, deployment metrics. Should recommend government agency letter from CDC contact. Should NOT trigger any of the 5 entrepreneur traps.

description: IT consulting firm in shortage occupation — should trigger Trap 1
input: Raj Patel, MS in Computer Science from Stanford (2016). Founded TechBridge Consulting LLC in 2022, providing cloud migration and cybersecurity consulting services to mid-market companies. 8 employees, all H-1B holders. Revenue: $1.2M. No patents. No publications. Claims: 'There is a critical shortage of cybersecurity professionals in the U.S. — my firm fills this gap.' No proprietary technology — uses standard AWS/Azure tools and frameworks.
expected_behavior: Should trigger Trap 1 (consulting in shortage occupation), Trap 3 (general job creation), and Trap 4 (benefits limited to specific employers). Should flag as BORDERLINE or NOT_QUALIFIED. Should recommend reframing toward a specific cybersecurity innovation if possible, or advise that this case is weak for NIW. Cybersecurity domain is Tier 1, but the ENDEAVOR is consulting, not innovation.

description: PhD holder in wrong occupation — should fail EB-2 gate
input: Dr. James Wilson, PhD in Chemistry from UC Berkeley (2015). Founded an upscale farm-to-table restaurant chain focused on sustainable sourcing. 3 locations, 45 employees. Revenue: $2.8M. Claims his chemistry background helps him understand food science. No patents related to food/restaurant industry. No publications in food science. No relevant certifications in food service management.
expected_behavior: Should FAIL EB-2 gate — 'baker/restaurateur does not typically require bachelor's degree for entry' per USCIS Policy Manual. PhD in chemistry is irrelevant because the occupation (restaurant management) doesn't require advanced degree as standard entry. Verdict: NOT_QUALIFIED on EB-2 gate. Should explain that the chemistry PhD cannot be leveraged for a restaurant endeavor.

description: Startup without detailed national impact — should trigger Trap 5
input: Sarah Kim, MS in Data Science from Columbia (2019). Founded DataPulse AI, an 'AI-powered analytics platform for small businesses.' 2 employees. Seed funding: $150K from friends and family. No external VC. Revenue: $40K. No patents. 1 paper in a workshop (not peer-reviewed). No government engagement. Claims: 'AI is transforming how businesses operate. Our platform democratizes AI for SMBs.' No specific problem quantified. No government program alignment.
expected_behavior: Should trigger Trap 2 (industry-level importance), Trap 5 (startup without detailed national impact). General BI/Analytics is Tier 3. Should flag as NOT_QUALIFIED or BORDERLINE. Should recommend: (1) identify a specific Tier 1-2 application domain, (2) quantify a specific national problem, (3) obtain external validation, (4) build publication/patent portfolio.
